channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Niger suspends nine French media bodies: Watchdog slams ‘abusive’ decision
item.description: Niger's military government has banned dozens of local and foreign reporters since seizing power in 2023.

item.title: Palestinians run West Bank freedom marathon along separation wall
item.description: Palestinians ran alongside the separation wall today, a structure that cuts them off from their land and their families.

item.title: One dead in US after being struck by taking off Frontier Airlines plane
item.description: Authorities say individual jumped fence at Denver International Airport in Colorado before being struck.

item.title: Israel to release two detained Gaza flotilla activists
item.description: Lawyer representing Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Avila tells Al Jazeera they will be 'released to their home countries'.

item.title: Bahrain rounds up dozens over alleged links to Iran’s IRGC
item.description: Interior Ministry says legal proceedings are under way against the 41 people arrested.

item.title: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s PM, ending Orban’s 16 years in power
item.description: Magyar's Tisza party secured a huge parliamentary majority in last month's election.

item.title: LIVE: Manchester City vs Brentford – Premier League
item.description: Follow our live build-up, with full team news coverage, ahead of our text commentary stream as City chase Arsenal.

item.title: ‘A year of resistance’: Cuba’s private sector faces Trump’s oil blockade
item.description: US oil blockade on Cuba is a significant blow to small family firms struggling with power outages and fuel shortages.

item.title: Iran says it will play at 2026 World Cup if hosts address ‘concerns’
item.description: Iran's presence at the tournament has been shrouded in uncertainty since the US and Israel launched war on the country.

item.title: Trump says hantavirus is ‘under control’ as WHO tracks cruise outbreak
item.description: US President Donald Trump says a hantavirus cruise ship cluster is ‘under very good control.’

item.title: Australia’s populist One Nation scores first-ever lower house victory
item.description: The far-right party's winning candidate, David Farley, has advocated for stricter migration and farming reforms.

item.title: Displaced Lebanese children keep learning amid displacement
item.description: A school in Sidon is now home to displaced families from southern Lebanon, but classes are still running.

item.title: US military kills two people in new eastern Pacific Ocean attack
item.description: The killings mark the third attack in May alone by the US military in the eastern Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Gaza at the Venice Biennale: Where language falls short, threads take over
item.description: I co-commissioned the Gaza Genocide Tapestry which will be displayed at the Venice Biennale. Here's the story behind it.

item.title: Scaled-back Victory Day parade held in Moscow
item.description: Russia marks downsized Victory Day parade as Putin pledges victory in Ukraine 'special military operation'.

item.title: World Cup 2026: How US football has evolved since hosting the 1994 event
item.description: In the 32 years since the United States last hosted the Cup, football has moved from the margins to the mainstream.

item.title: Barcelona vs Real Madrid: La Liga – preview, team news, start and lineups
item.description: Real Madrid desperate to beat bitter rivals Barca, who only need a draw in the Clasico to be crowned champions again.

item.title: The war on Iran will likely end in American retreat
item.description: The American empire cannot win the war against Iran at acceptable financial, military, and political costs.

item.title: Senior Sri Lankan monk arrested for alleged child sex crimes
item.description: Pallegama Hemarathana is accused of abusing an 11-year-old girl in a temple.

item.title: Hacked educational platform partially restored for millions of students
item.description: The hacker group, ShinyHunters, threatened to leak student data after breaching the educational platform Canvas.

item.title: Russia holds downsized Victory Day parade
item.description: Putin invoked the Soviet victory in World War II to rally his army in Ukraine, saying 'our heroes move forward'.

item.title: Spain’s Canary Islands brace for incoming hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
item.description: The 140 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius will be 'completely isolated' and evacuated, say Spanish authorities.

item.title: Israeli settlers set fire to homes and cars in violent West Bank raids
item.description: Dozens of Israeli settlers stormed various areas of the West Bank, set cars on fire, and attacked Palestinians.

item.title: Financial struggles burden Yemen’s army as soldiers wait for wages
item.description: Yemeni soldiers earn $38 to $116 monthly, but an unstable currency has eroded the value of their salaries.

item.title: Wembanyama powers Spurs past T-Wolves as Knicks beat Sixers in NBA playoffs
item.description: San Antonio take 2-1 semifinal series lead over Minnesota, while New York Knicks are 3-0 up against Philadelphia 76ers.
